title: rubus hedycarpus subsp. dynatos
intro: Rubus hedycarpus subsp. dynatos, commonly known as the Southern dewberry, is a type of wild shrub that belongs to the rose family and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus hedycarpus subsp. dynatos include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to make jams and jellies, although its medicinal properties are typically extracted through solvent-based preparations.
